site.title: NazivIER – IER researcher-title calculator
site.description: Internal tool of the Institute for Economic Research (IER, Ljubljana) for automatic calculation of researcher titles under the new rulebook, powered by SICRIS and OpenAlex.
site.ogDescription: Automatic title-eligibility check under the IER researcher-titles rulebook (draft 26 May 2026). Bibliography from SICRIS, citations from OpenAlex.

header.eyebrow: Institute for Economic Research · internal tool
header.title: NazivIER – researcher-title calculator
header.subtitle1: Automatic eligibility check against the new
header.subtitle2: (draft 26 May 2026), Annexes 2 and 3. Bibliography from SICRIS, citations from SICRIS WoS plus OpenAlex (covers WoS, Scopus, Crossref).

picker.pasteUrl: or 2. Paste a SICRIS URL / enter an ID
picker.invalidInput: Enter a valid SICRIS ID (e.g. 33182) or profile URL.
picker.rosterError: Could not load the list ({msg}). Use the field on the right to enter a SICRIS ID manually.

loading: Fetching bibliography from SICRIS, citations from SICRIS WoS + OpenAlex, and projects from ier.si … (a few seconds on first request, cached after).

summary.openAlexNotFound: OpenAlex record not found – please verify the name

metadata.title: Data not exposed by public sources
metadata.subtitle: Bibliography, citations and projects are fetched automatically (SICRIS, OpenAlex, ier.si). The fields below are required by the rulebook but not always available in public databases – enter or confirm manually.

fields.leadershipFte: Pogoj 3 (a–d): cumulative value of completed led projects (FTE A)
fields.leadershipYears: Pogoj 3 (e): years in an executive function
fields.leadershipYearsHelp: Only the five rulebook-enumerated functions count: director, chair of the management board, chair of the scientific council, head of programme group, head of infrastructure group (at the Institute or an equivalent institution). Project leadership years do NOT belong here.
fields.leadershipYearsTooltip: At least 1 / 2 / 3 years holding an executive function: director, chair of management board, chair of scientific council, head of programme group, head of infrastructure group.
fields.weight10Count: Weight-1.0 achievements without typology (completed PhD mentorships, Zois/international awards)
fields.weight05Count: Weight-0.5 achievements without typology (journal editorial board, completed leadership of research project/programme)
fields.reelectionHelp: Tick if this is a re-election to the same title – the rulebook then requires at least half of every numeric threshold since the previous election.
fields.additionalDepositsHelp: OpenAlex detected {deposited} of {total} post-2024 publications as open access ({missing} missing). If you have already deposited – but OpenAlex has not yet indexed – or will deposit additional works in the institutional / thematic repository before the application is reviewed, enter the count here. The evaluator recomputes the ratio against the 100 % threshold in Article 11(6). This never bypasses the rule, it records the realistic state.
fields.ierLeadershipLabel: Project leadership from ier.si (audit only — does NOT auto-fill Pogoj 3)
fields.ierLeadershipHelp: From the IER website we detected the candidate leads {count} projects ({external} outside pure ARIS funding), spanning about {years} years. Projects for audit:
fields.ierLeadershipDisclaimer: Important: project-leadership years do NOT by themselves satisfy Pogoj 3 path (e). The rulebook requires years holding an executive function (director, chair of management board, chair of scientific council, head of programme group, head of infrastructure group). These projects are surfaced solely as audit evidence and can support manually entered FTE values for Pogoj 3 (a–d) or Pogoj 2 (value of non-ARIS projects).
fields.authorshipPerPubHelp: The SICRIS bibliography does not expose who was first, lead or corresponding author, so every entry defaults to 0.7 (equal). Tag your actual role – the evaluator recomputes the publication equivalents. This is the single biggest accuracy lever.

breakdownGroups.showTitles: {count, plural, one {# title} other {# titles}} in group
breakdownGroups.openHint: Click to see {count, plural, one {# title} other {# titles}}

osDemo.title: Demo mode for the Open-Science requirement (Article 11, paragraph 6):
osDemo.body: Article 11(6) is new and in this demo it is treated as fulfilled for every researcher because the rulebook is still a draft (proposal of 26 May 2026). The true open-access status is still computed and displayed on each title card and in the summary strip so candidates can see how many deposits separate them from 100 % coverage.

duplicates.title: Possible duplicates in the bibliography:
duplicates.summary: {groups, plural, one {# title} other {# titles}} appear more than once ({records} records, ~{eq} equivalents combined). The algorithm still counts each record separately – this is a reviewer hint, not a score adjustment.
duplicates.disclaimer: The rulebook (Annex 3) sums every COBISS record at its own weight, so this list is informational only. During qualitative review (Article 12) the commission can decide whether duplicate records represent genuinely distinct outputs (conference paper + journal version, SL + EN translation, preprint + final) or a cataloguing issue – COBISS cataloguing hygiene is the librarian's responsibility, not the calculator's.

diagnostics.algoTooltip: Algorithm / rulebook version driving the calculation
diagnostics.detailsSummary: Technical details for a bug report
diagnostics.detailsHelp: Copy the block below and include it in a bug report. It contains the version, researcher ID, timestamp and error details. No personal data.

evalCard.contributionsSummary: {eq} equivalents from {count, plural, one {# publication} other {# publications}}
evalCard.reelectionTooltip: Article 22(2): on re-election to the same title the candidate must meet at least half of each first-election threshold.

equivalent.head: Achievement equivalent
equivalent.body: = achievement weight × authorship factor (Rulebook, Annex 3, Notes on criteria).

weights.head: Weights
weights.body: follow Annex 3: 1.0 for Q1/Q2 articles (1A1/1A2 in COBISS), monographs, patents; 0.7 for other peer-reviewed articles; 0.5 for invited abstracts and professional monographs; 0.3 for professional articles and reports; 0.1 for popular contributions.

q12.head: Q1/Q2 status
q12.body: is determined primarily via an embedded SCImago JR snapshot keyed by journal ISSN-L (economics, finance and adjacent journals). When the ISSN is not in the snapshot, we fall back to a heuristic: 1.01/1.02 publications are ranked by impact, the top quartile is tagged Q1, the next Q2.

authorship.head: Authorship factor
authorship.body: : SICRIS bibliography export does not expose the corresponding author, so default is 0.7 (equal). Options are first/lead/corresponding (1.0), equal (0.7) and other co-authorship (0.3). The per-publication role can be overridden in the panel above.

citations.head: Clean citations
citations.body: : the official number for the rulebook check is SICRIS WoS (»čisti citati po WoS« – what ARIS evaluators see – fetched with a two-step call to bib.cobiss.net/biblioweb/cit/…). We also display OpenAlex (covers WoS + Scopus + Crossref + PubMed) as the broader-coverage diagnostic, with self-citations stripped via authorships.author.id matching.

leadership.head: Leadership capability (Pogoj 3)
leadership.body: : the rulebook (Annex 3) offers two alternative paths. (a–d) Cumulative value of completed led projects ≥ 1 / 5 / 10 FTE category A (by career stage) – entered manually, because ier.si does not publish FTE. (e) At least 1 / 2 / 3 years holding an executive function: director, chair of the management board, chair of the scientific council, head of programme group, head of infrastructure group. Years of project leadership (from ier.si/projekti) do NOT qualify for path (e); they are shown as an audit list under the metadata panel, never auto-filled.

openScience.head: Open Science (Article 11, paragraph 6)
openScience.body: : the Open-Science requirement is new in the IER rulebook (draft of 26 May 2026, Article 11(6)–(8)). It explicitly transposes the Slovenian Ordinance on the conduct of scientific-research work (Official Gazette RS, 59/23). In this demo we therefore treat it as fulfilled for every researcher – while the rulebook is still a proposal we do not want to block eligibility. The true OA status is still computed (OpenAlex `open_access.is_oa`) and displayed so candidates see how far they are from 100 % coverage.

earlyPromotion.head: Early promotion (Article 14, paragraph 5)
earlyPromotion.body: : we flag candidates who exceed the minimum thresholds by ≥ 50 % AND meet the years-in-research-sector floor (≥ 10 yrs for stage III, ≥ 15 yrs for stage IV). Shown as a badge on each title card.

reelection.head: Re-election (Article 22, paragraph 2)
reelection.body: : when you tick »re-election« in the meta panel, every numeric threshold is halved – the rulebook requires at least half of the first-election values.

footer: Methodology per the rulebook of 26 May 2026, Annex 3. Typology per the COBISS typology of documents/works. Citations: SICRIS WoS (official) + OpenAlex (broader coverage). Authorship factor defaults to 0.7 (equal) – set to 1.0 per publication for first / lead / corresponding author.

kajaCalibration.lockTooltip: Calibration view (internal) – click to enter password
kajaCalibration.modalIntro: Enter the password to display the strict Σ next to the rulebook Σ (informational only, does not affect pass/fail).
kajaCalibration.spec: Strict interpretation: authorship factor = 1 only for first/corresponding authorships and awards (weight 1.0); all other authorship = 0. Typology weights remain as in the Rulebook.
kajaCalibration.strictHint: Alternative calculation counting only first/corresponding authors plus awards.
kajaCalibration.deltaAbove: Strict Σ is above the rulebook Σ.
kajaCalibration.deltaBelow: Strict Σ is below the rulebook Σ.
kajaCalibration.limit_authorship: The authorship factor comes from the role manually set on each publication (default equal 0.7); before trusting the strict Σ, review the roles in the Metadata section.
kajaCalibration.limit_senior: »Lead« author is not separated from »first/corresponding« in this app; if the researcher is a senior author (last in the list), mark the publication as »first/lead/corresponding« manually.
kajaCalibration.limit_corresponding: Corresponding author is not auto-detected from SICRIS – mark manually on publications where the researcher is the corresponding author.
kajaCalibration.limit_awards: Enter awards and recognitions manually in the Metadata section (»Additional achievements – weight 1.0«).
kajaCalibration.limit_q12: 1.01/1.02 without a Q1/Q2 tag count at weight 0.7. For Q1/Q2 mark them manually in the Metadata section to get the full weight 1.0.
